healthCheckProcedure.title: Vehicle Diagnostic Scan Procedure
healthCheckProcedure.baseLaborDescription: Base scan time typically covers initial connection and code retrieval
healthCheckProcedure.notIncludedNote: Additional diagnostic work requires separate billing

steps.description: Position vehicle in diagnostic bay
Activate hazard lights to maintain module communication
Ensure vehicle reaches proper operating temperature
Access battery terminals (may require component removal)
Connect battery maintainer/support device
steps.notes: Standard charger acceptable for basic scans. ECU programming requires professional-grade power supply connected 10+ minutes before procedure.

steps.description: Connect diagnostic tool and initiate scan
Record all diagnostic trouble codes
Capture freeze frame data, timestamps, and key cycle information
Review live data streams
Check ADAS sensor alignment values (radar, camera, BSM)
Verify steering angle sensor readings
Research DTCs using manufacturer resources
Follow OEM diagnostic flowcharts for fault isolation
Perform live testing to confirm active faults
Complete necessary repairs
Perform in-process verification scan
Complete required calibrations and system resets
Road test to complete drive cycles and verify repairs
Final post-repair scan to confirm code clearance
Document results and secure equipment

batterySupport.title: Proper Battery Support for Diagnostics

notBatterySupport: Portable jump starters do not provide stable voltage
Basic trickle chargers cannot maintain consistent power
reasons: Unregulated charging can damage sensitive electronics
Portable units may lose charge during extended procedures
criticalInfo.oemRecommended: Many manufacturers specify professional-grade power supplies (e.g., Midtronics-type units)

whyBatterySupportMatters: Low voltage causes the vehicle to disable non-essential systems to preserve starting ability
Diagnostic tools may fail to communicate with all modules when voltage drops

comparison.oem: Continuous updates as issues are discovered
comparison.aftermarket: Typically updated quarterly or less frequently

comparison.oem: Direct access to factory repair procedures
comparison.aftermarket: May use third-party repair databases with varying accuracy
Coverage may lag 12-18 months behind new releases

comparison.oem: Knows exact vehicle equipment from factory records
comparison.aftermarket: May generate false codes for equipment not installed on that specific vehicle

comparison.oem: Full access to protected vehicle systems
comparison.aftermarket: Many newer vehicles block third-party access to certain modules

comparison.oem: Accurate module count based on build data
comparison.aftermarket: Module counts may vary; some functions like ADAS calibration may be limited

comparison.oem: Shows current vs stored codes with cycle counts
comparison.aftermarket: May not capture all historical fault information

comparison.oem: Complete snapshot data including date, time, mileage
comparison.aftermarket: Freeze frame data may be incomplete for some systems

aftermarketScanToolQuestions.title: Questions for Third-Party Tool Vendors
questions: Can your tool access the vehicle's factory build data?
How does your tool determine which modules to test without build data?
Can your tool detect connected vehicle service subscriptions?
Will your tool generate codes for optional equipment not installed on a specific vehicle?
What is the newest model year your tool fully supports?

scanningMyths.title: Addressing Scan Pricing Disputes
scanningMyths.myth: Insurer offers flat rate for scanning regardless of work performed

responses.action: Request itemization of what their rate covers
Document each step of your scan procedure
Bill separately for DTC research time
Include safety system inspections when applicable

fourKeyQuestions.title: Determining Billable Operations
fourKeyQuestions.description: Ask these questions for every operation

questions.question: Is this operation necessary to restore safe, pre-loss condition?
Is this work included in another labor operation, or is it standalone?
Does a published labor time exist for this operation?
If no published time exists, what is the reasonable time/value?

colors.action: Investigate cause; may clear after drive cycles

common.description: Seat weight sensors and position detection systems
Cameras, radar, and ultrasonic sensors for driver assistance
Bluetooth, wireless charging, and infotainment pairing
Liftgate, sliding door, and trunk position limits
Backup camera, surround view, and mirror cameras
Rear corner radar and cross-traffic sensors
Programming replacement control units with vehicle data

oem.name: OEM Position Statements
oem.description: Manufacturer guidance on repair procedures and requirements
